channel.title: PDB on Welcome to Christophe Nasarre's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in PDB on Welcome to Christophe Nasarre's Blog

item.title: How to support .NET Framework PDB format and source line with ISymUnmanagedReader
item.description: After DIA and DbgHelp, time to dig into ISymUnmanagedReader to get its line in source code.

item.title: But where is my method code? DbgHelp comes to the rescue
item.description: This post show how DbgHelp could help you figure out the line and source code of each managed methods from a .pdb file

item.title: How to dump function symbols from a .pdb file
item.description: Detail how to write a tool that generates a .sym file listing functions symbols with their address, size and signature from a .pdb file

item.title: Vibe coding a .pdb dumper or how I became a Product Manager
item.description: Follow me Vibe Coding in Cursor during my Datadog R&D Week to list function symbols with their signature and more

item.title: How to monitor .NET applications startup
item.description: This episode explains how to monitor the startup of a .NET application and get insights about its lock and wait contentions duration

item.title: Measuring the impact of locks and waits on latency in your .NET apps
item.description: Monitor mutex, semaphore and event wait duration

item.title: Crap: the application is randomly crashing!
item.description: This post is listing which steps were followed to investigate a customer random crash issue I faced last week.

item.title: Troubleshooting CPU and exceptions issues with Datadog toolbox
item.description: Learn how to use Datadog CPU and exceptions profiling to troubleshoot well known Tess Ferrandez BuggyBits

item.title: Build your own .NET CPU profiler in C#
item.description: After describing memory allocation profiling it is now time to dig into the CPU sample profiling in C#!

item.title: Build your own .NET memory profiler in C# — call stacks (2/2–2)
item.description: In this last episode I detail how to transform addresses from the stack into methods name and signature.

item.title: Build your own .NET memory profiler in C# — call stacks (2/2–1)
item.description: This post explains how to get the call stack corresponding to the allocations with CLR events.

item.title: Let’s debug the Core CLR with WinDBG!
item.description: This post of the series shows how you could easily debug the Core CLR in a real world case of insane thread contention duration.
